steps.title: Read circle M
steps.content:
In a circle equation, the right side is $r^2$. For circle $M$:
$$(x-4)^2+(y+6)^2=36$$
so the radius is:
$$\sqrt{36}=6$$

steps.title: Double the radius
steps.content:
Circle $N$ has twice the radius of circle $M$:
$$2(6)=12$$

steps.title: Square the new radius
steps.content:
The constant $k$ is the square of circle $N$'s radius:
$$k=12^2=144$$
The answer is 144.
